edge_hypothesis: RSI pullback was ACCEPTED on 4h (PF=1.21-1.33, 38 trades) but remains the most fragile strategy in the portfolio due to low trade count. The advisory's #1 validation directive: test on 1h for cross-timeframe validation. The 1h timeframe should generate 3-4x more RSI pullback opportunities because (a) 4x more bars per day and (b) pullbacks are faster/more frequent on lower timeframes. The critical insight is that MACD went from good-on-4h to excellent-on-1h (PF=1.03→1.46 at extreme R:R). The same pattern should apply to RSI pullback: 4h at 7:1 produced PF=1.33 with 38 trades, so 1h at similar R:R should produce 100-150 trades while maintaining the edge. We test three approaches: (1) deep pullback (RSI<35) with extreme R:R to catch only the strongest trend continuations, (2) faster EMA periods (100/30 instead of 200/50) for more responsive trend detection on 1h noise, and (3) shallow pullback (RSI<45) with moderate R:R for maximum trade count. The RSI pullback signal is fundamentally different from MACD and Supertrend — it buys weakness in a strong trend rather than buying momentum shifts. If this edge persists on 1h, we have THREE uncorrelated signal types for portfolio construction. Regime: TRENDING (EMA trend confirmation required). Invalidation: if PF < 1.0 on 1h across all variants, the RSI pullback edge is timeframe-specific to 4h and the 38-trade sample was overfit.

variants.name: rsi_pullback_1h_deep_trend_8to1
variants.description: Deep RSI pullback (RSI<35 for long, RSI>65 for short) on ETH/1h with EMA200 trend filter and 8:1 R:R. The quality variant: only enters on genuine deep pullbacks where RSI reaches oversold territory within an established trend. The deep threshold (35 vs default 40) means we're buying significant dips, not shallow noise. 8:1 R:R matches what made Supertrend achieve PF=1.91. Trade count will be lower (~40-60) but each entry is a high-conviction pullback-to-trend continuation.
entry_long: Close > EMA_200 confirms established bullish trend.
EMA_50 slope is rising (current > previous bar) confirms trend momentum.
RSI_14 <= 35 confirms deep pullback — price has pulled back significantly into trend.
entry_short: Close < EMA_200 confirms established bearish trend.
EMA_50 slope is falling (current < previous bar) confirms trend momentum.
RSI_14 >= 65 confirms deep pullback — price has bounced significantly into downtrend.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
RSI deep pullback threshold: 35/65 for quality-over-quantity
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (8.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if trades < 20 on 1h, RSI<35 is too restrictive even with 4x more bars
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 8.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 for trend direction + EMA_50 slope for momentum
RSI_14 <= 35/>=65 — deep pullback threshold for high-conviction entries
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 8.0x TP — extreme R:R to ride trend continuations

variants.name: rsi_pullback_1h_fast_ema_6to1
variants.description: RSI pullback on ETH/1h with FASTER EMA periods (EMA100 trend, EMA30 slope) and 6:1 R:R. The adaptive variant: standard 200/50 EMAs are designed for 4h timeframe. On 1h, a 200-period EMA spans ~8 days vs ~33 days on 4h — it may be too slow or too fast depending on perspective. Testing EMA100/30 (4 days trend, 30 hours slope) for a more responsive trend filter suited to 1h dynamics. Standard RSI threshold (40/60) for balanced trade generation. 6:1 R:R is the proven MACD 1h ratio.
entry_long: Close > EMA_100 confirms bullish trend (faster period for 1h responsiveness).
EMA_30 slope is rising (current > previous bar) confirms near-term momentum.
entry_short: Close < EMA_100 confirms bearish trend (faster period for 1h responsiveness).
EMA_30 slope is falling (current < previous bar) confirms near-term momentum.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Faster EMA periods for 1h: trend=100, slope=30
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (6.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < PF of standard 200/50 variant, faster EMAs capture noise not signal
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 6.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_100 trend + EMA_30 slope — faster periods tuned for 1h timeframe dynamics
RSI_14 <= 40/>=60 — standard threshold, balanced trade generation
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 6.0x TP — proven MACD 1h R:R ratio

variants.name: rsi_pullback_1h_shallow_volume_5to1
variants.description: Shallow RSI pullback (RSI<45 long, RSI>55 short) on ETH/1h with EMA200 trend and 5:1 R:R. The high-frequency variant: relaxed RSI threshold (45 vs default 40) captures more pullbacks including shallow retracements. This maximizes trade count — critical for statistical significance on 1h. The 5:1 R:R is moderate by current standards but still a fee-killer. If this variant produces 150+ trades with PF>1.05, it validates the RSI pullback edge at scale. Wider stop (2.0x) per mandatory safe floor.
entry_long: Close > EMA_200 confirms established bullish trend.
EMA_50 slope is rising (current > previous bar) confirms trend momentum.
RSI_14 <= 45 captures shallow-to-medium pullbacks within trend.
entry_short: Close < EMA_200 confirms established bearish trend.
EMA_50 slope is falling (current < previous bar) confirms trend momentum.
RSI_14 >= 55 captures shallow-to-medium pullbacks within downtrend.
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Shallow RSI threshold: 45/55 for maximum trade count
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (5.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 1.0, shallow RSI (45) admits too much noise — tighten to 40 or 35
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 5.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 trend + EMA_50 slope — standard periods, proven on 4h
RSI_14 <= 45/>=55 — relaxed threshold for maximum trade count on 1h
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 5.0x TP — moderate fee-killer for high trade count
